evals.prompt: Write a Playwright test for our Stripe checkout that covers a successful Visa payment plus a declined card and an insufficient-funds card. Assert the right outcome for each.
Our checkout uses a 3D Secure card that always challenges. Write the Playwright steps to fill the card, trigger the 3DS challenge, and complete authentication so the payment succeeds.
expected_patterns: Complete authentication OR Complete OR authorize

evals.prompt: I need to test that an annual subscription renewal charges the customer 12 months later without waiting a year. How do I simulate the billing cycle?
expected_patterns: test clock OR test_clock OR test-clock
anti_patterns: fake timers OR sinon OR jest.useFakeTimers

evals.prompt: Set up local webhook testing for our Stripe integration so events from a test payment reach our /webhooks endpoint on localhost:3000.
expected_patterns: whsec_ OR signing secret OR webhook secret
anti_patterns: poll the api OR polling for status

evals.prompt: Write the webhook handler test that verifies the Stripe signature and rejects a forged or tampered event.
expected_patterns: raw body OR rawBody OR req.body.*raw OR express.raw

evals.prompt: Stripe retries webhooks and we saw a customer get fulfilled twice. Write a test proving our handler is idempotent when the same event is delivered twice.
expected_patterns: UNIQUE OR unique constraint OR already processed
deliver.*twice OR send.*same event OR retry
expect.*once OR called.*1 OR fulfilled once
anti_patterns: idempotency key on the request only
in-memory set OR global variable for dedup

evals.prompt: Test a failed recurring payment: the renewal fails, the subscription goes past_due, then a refund is issued. How do I drive this end to end?
expected_patterns: test clock OR test_clock OR advance
anti_patterns: delete the subscription instead of refund

evals.prompt: We just added PayPal and Adyen alongside Stripe. What is the general sandbox pattern for testing these PSPs, and what stays the same versus Stripe?
expected_patterns: Adyen.*test card OR Adyen test OR test_card_number
PayPal.*sandbox OR sandbox account OR buyer account
no real card OR test credentials OR test mode
Braintree OR drop-in OR hosted fields
anti_patterns: use the same Stripe test cards for adyen OR same test cards across psp

evals.prompt: Code review: a teammate hardcoded a real corporate card number into a checkout test that runs in CI. What's wrong and what should it use instead?
expected_patterns: never.*real card OR no real PAN OR PCI
test mode OR pk_test OR sk_test
secret OR remove.*from.*repo OR env OR rotate
anti_patterns: mask the card in logs OR just redact

evals.prompt: Write a reconciliation test that confirms a successful checkout produces a PaymentIntent with status succeeded and that our DB order is marked paid only after the payment_intent.succeeded webhook, not on redirect.
expected_patterns: not.*redirect OR after webhook OR not on return_url
anti_patterns: mark paid on redirect OR fulfill on return_url
